metaTitle: Coin Flipper & Dice Roller — Online Random Decision Maker
metaDescription: Flip a coin or roll multiple dice (D4-D20) instantly. Perfect for board games, TRPGs, and fair decision making. 100% private and works offline.
introSummary: Our professional-grade Coin Flipper and Dice Roller is designed for fairness and versatility. Whether you're settling a friendly dispute with a 50/50 heads or tails toss, or managing a complex Tabletop RPG (TRPG) session with D20 rolls, our tool provides cryptographically secure randomness. Built with a 'Privacy-First' approach, every flip and roll happens entirely on your device, ensuring a fast, ad-free, and secure experience.

contextUsage.title: For Board Games & TRPGs
contextUsage.desc: Replace missing physical dice for Dungeons & Dragons (D&D), Monopoly, or Catan. Supports D4, D6, D8, D10, D12, and D20.

contextUsage.title: For Fair Decision Making
contextUsage.desc: Settle arguments or make quick choices between two options (Heads or Tails) with a provably fair 50/50 chance.

contextUsage.title: For Statistics & Probability
contextUsage.desc: Track your flip history and see real-time statistics to understand probability distributions over multiple trials.

howto.description: Toggle between the 'Coin Flip' for binary decisions or 'Dice Roller' for games and complex randomizations.
Choose your dice type (from D4 to D20) and select the quantity. You can roll up to 10 dice simultaneously for bulk results.
Tap the center area or click 'Flip/Roll'. Our engine uses browser-level entropy for true random results.
Check the history log below to see previous outcomes, total sums of dice rolls, and heads/tails ratios.

why: Cryptographically Secure — Uses 'Math.random()' built into your browser's engine for unbiased, fair results.
Full Polyhedral Support — Includes all standard RPG dice: D4, D6, D8, D10, D12, and the essential D20.
Multi-Dice Logic — Need to roll 3D6? Our tool handles multiple dice at once and provides both individual results and a total sum.
Instant & Offline — No server lag or internet required. Perfect for gaming sessions in remote areas or low-connectivity environments.
Zero Tracking — We value your privacy. Your decision-making data and roll results are never uploaded or stored on our servers.
Stat Tracker — Built-in counter to show how many times Heads or Tails has appeared during your current session.

faq.a: Yes. While physical coins can have slight weight biases, our digital coin uses a random number generator that ensures an exact 50% probability over the long term.

faq.q: Can I use this for Dungeons & Dragons?
faq.a: Absolutely! We provide the full set of polyhedral dice (D4-D20) required for D&D, Pathfinder, and other d20-system tabletop games.

faq.q: How many dice can I roll at once?
faq.a: You can roll multiple dice of the same type simultaneously. The tool will display each individual die result and the calculated total sum.

faq.q: Does the 'Heads or Tails' result get saved?
faq.a: The history is only saved in your current browser session. Once you refresh or close the tab, all session data is permanently cleared.

faq.q: Why use a digital roller instead of physical dice?
faq.a: Digital rollers are immune to 'loaded dice' bias, can't be lost under the sofa, and allow for instant calculation of large dice pools (like 10D6).

technicalSpecs.useCase: Ensures unbiased results for gaming and gambling simulations
Full compatibility with all major tabletop RPG systems
Instantaneous results even when rolling 10+ dice at once
Optimized to run smoothly on older smartphones during long game sessions

troubleshooting: Dice not rolling? Ensure JavaScript is enabled in your browser, as all calculations happen on the client side.
History missing? If you refresh the page or clear your cache, the local session history will be reset.
Total sum incorrect? Note that some dice (like D10) are indexed 0-9 or 1-10 depending on the system; our D10 uses the standard 1-10 range.
proTips: If you're stuck between two restaurants, assign 'Heads' to one and 'Tails' to the other for a stress-free choice.
Tabletop GMs: Use the D20 roller for 'Secret Rolls' on your phone to keep the tension high at the table.
For probability students, flip the coin 100 times and check the history to see the Law of Large Numbers in action.
